title: Fix Code Rendering to Show Full Mapping with Source Objects
story: As a data migration specialist, I want the SQL code output to show the full source-to-target mapping including source object names, so that the generated code accurately reflects my field mapping work.
description:
Fix two issues with the SQL code rendering:

1. **Limited record count**: The code output was only showing a limited number of fields instead of all mapped records.
2. **Missing source object name**: The rendered SQL was only including the Salesforce field name without the mapped source object. The correct format should be `"SourceObject"."SourceField" AS "TargetField"` (e.g., `"SimpleLead"."Last Name" AS "LastName"`).

The mapping data table shows the relationship between target fields and source fields (including the source object), and this full relationship needs to be reflected in the code output.
acceptance_criteria: All mapped fields are included in the SQL output, not just a limited subset
Source object name is included in the field reference (e.g., '"SimpleLead"."Last Name"')
Unmapped fields are still included with just the Salesforce field name
ux_changes: SQL code output now displays all fields and includes the full source object path in field references.
middleware_changes: SQL generation query updated to include source object names and remove the record limit that was truncating output.
implementation_notes: Developer noted this may have been partially addressed in MBT-255. The record truncation was a query limit issue. The source object name was already available in the mapping data but was not being included in the SQL generation logic.
comment_summary: Developer needed clarification on the format for appending mapped field names. Screenshots were provided showing the expected format. Developer noted the source object inclusion was already covered in MBT-255 work. Clarification requested on the 'limited records' issue.
